headline: The UK’s best winter walks
summary: Blow away the cobwebs, from County Antrim to the Cotswolds
body_text:
Bourton-on-the-Water, affectionately known as the “Venice of the Cotswolds” is a picturesque village nestled along the tranquil River Windrush. Renowned for its vibrant café culture – with nearly a dozen cosy spots to choose from – the village is an ideal starting and finishing point for a countryside stroll, especially in the crisp, quieter winter months. This delightful five-mile circular walk takes you north through rolling fields and postcard-perfect landscapes, leading to the enchanting village of Lower Slaughter. At around the halfway mark, stop at The Slaughters Country Inn for some hearty refreshment or a warm drink by the fire. With its mix of gentle terrain and scenic beauty, this route caters to both seasoned hikers and casual walkers.
